title: Essential Connectors
description: Fundamental transitions for BUEPT Essay writing.

cards.definition: In addition to what has already been said.
cards.example: Furthermore, the economic impact cannot be ignored.

cards.definition: In spite of; despite.
cards.example: Notwithstanding the initial setbacks, the project succeeded.

cards.definition: To the extent that; because.
cards.example: Inasmuch as he is a taxpayer, he has a right to know.

cards.definition: As a result.
cards.example: The experiment failed; consequently, we began anew.

cards.definition: In addition; also.
cards.example: The roof leaks; moreover, the walls are damp.

title: Academic Essay Verbs
description: Sophisticated verbs for argumentation and analysis.

cards.definition: To emphasize or show the importance of something.
cards.example: These findings underscore the need for further research.

cards.definition: To confirm or support with evidence.
cards.example: Recent studies corroborate his theory.

cards.definition: To restrain oneself from doing or enjoying something.
cards.example: Members must abstain from voting on this issue.

cards.definition: To make a problem or bad situation worse.
cards.example: His comments only served to exacerbate the conflict.

cards.definition: To make something less severe or painful.
cards.example: Steps were taken to mitigate the environmental damage.

title: Sociology & Society
description: Terms related to social structures, norms, and behavior.

cards.definition: The arrangement or classification of something into different groups.
cards.example: Social stratification remains a major focus of modern sociology.

cards.definition: The process of taking in and fully understanding information or ideas.
cards.example: The government promoted the assimilation of immigrants into the local culture.

cards.definition: Establishing, relating to, or deriving from a standard or norm.
cards.example: The study explores the normative influences on adolescent behavior.

cards.definition: Leadership or dominance, especially by one country or social group.
cards.example: The movie explores the cultural hegemony of Western media.

cards.definition: The action or process of converting something from religious to secular possession or use.
cards.example: Many experts point to the secularization of European society in the 20th century.

title: Technology & AI
description: Cutting-edge terms for modern tech discussions.

cards.definition: A process or set of rules to be followed in calculations or other problem-solving operations.
cards.example: The new search algorithm identifies relevant content more efficiently.

cards.definition: The use of largely automatic equipment in a system of manufacturing or other production process.
cards.example: Increased automation in the car industry has led to job losses.

cards.definition: Present, appearing, or found everywhere.
cards.example: Mobile phones are now ubiquitous in modern society.

cards.definition: Innovative technology that significantly alters the way that consumers, industries, or businesses operate.
cards.example: Artificial intelligence is often described as a disruptive technology.

cards.definition: The basic physical and organizational structures and facilities (e.g., buildings, roads, power supplies) needed for the operation of a society or enterprise.
cards.example: The city's digital infrastructure requires significant investment.

title: Environment & Ecology
description: Essential terms for climate and sustainability topics.

cards.definition: The ability to be maintained at a certain rate or level.
cards.example: Corporate sustainability is becoming a key metric for investors.

cards.definition: Reduction in the number or quantity of something.
cards.example: The rapid depletion of fossil fuels is a global concern.

cards.definition: The variety of life in the world or in a particular habitat or ecosystem.
cards.example: Protecting biodiversity is crucial for maintaining ecosystem health.

cards.definition: The production and discharge of something, especially gas or radiation.
cards.example: The treaty aims to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by 20%.

cards.definition: The state of being aware of and responsive to one's surroundings.
cards.example: There is a growing environmental consciousness among young consumers.
